name: Background Table
description: Five Parsecs From Home character background table (pp.24-25)

1-4.name: Peaceful High-Tech Colony
1-4.description: Grew up on a prosperous, technologically advanced world with excellent education.

5-9.name: Giant Overcrowded Dystopian City
5-9.description: Raised in the crushing urban sprawl, learned to move fast to survive.

10-13.name: Low-Tech Colony
10-13.description: From a frontier world with minimal technology, but practical survival skills.

14-17.name: Mining Colony
14-17.description: Hard labor in the mines built endurance and resilience.

18-21.name: Military Brat
18-21.description: Grew up on military bases, learned to handle weapons early.

22-25.name: Space Station
22-25.description: Raised in the artificial environment of an orbital habitat.

26-29.name: Military Outpost
26-29.description: Lived on the frontier of civilization, always alert for danger.

30-34.name: Drifter
30-34.description: Spent youth moving from place to place, picking up useful items.

35-39.name: Lower Megacity Class
35-39.description: Grew up in the dangerous lower levels, learned to defend yourself.

40-42.name: Wealthy Merchant Family
40-42.description: Born into wealth, with access to resources and connections.

43-46.name: Frontier Gang
43-46.description: Ran with a rough crowd on the frontier, learned to fight.

47-49.name: Religious Cult
47-49.description: Raised in a tight-knit religious community with shared purpose.

50-52.name: War-Torn Hell-Hole
50-52.description: Survived in a war zone, quick reflexes kept you alive.

53-55.name: Tech Guild
53-55.description: Trained by a prestigious technical guild with access to advanced equipment.

56-59.name: Subjugated Colony on Alien World
56-59.description: Lived under alien rule, learned to acquire useful tech.

60-64.name: Long-Term Space Mission
60-64.description: Spent formative years on a deep space vessel, gaining technical skills.

65-68.name: Research Outpost
65-68.description: Grew up among scientists and researchers, with access to experimental tech.

69-72.name: Primitive or Regressed World
69-72.description: From a world that has lost or rejected technology.

73-76.name: Orphan Utility Program
73-76.description: Raised in a state program, has connections to institutional support.

77-80.name: Isolationist Enclave
77-80.description: From a secretive community with knowledge of hidden opportunities.

81-84.name: Comfortable Megacity Class
81-84.description: Grew up in reasonable comfort with access to education and resources.

85-89.name: Industrial World
85-89.description: Raised among factories and manufacturing, familiar with equipment.

90-93.name: Bureaucrat
90-93.description: Family worked in administration, learned to navigate systems.

94-97.name: Wasteland Nomads
94-97.description: Traveled the wastelands with a nomadic group, always watchful.

98-100.name: Alien Culture
98-100.description: Raised among aliens, with access to their advanced technology.
